title: Recover missing browser validation affordance
user_request: Add an empty-state message to the todo list and make sure the message disappears after the first item is added.

harness_gap.description: The worker can run unit tests but has no browser automation or screenshot path, so it cannot observe the UI failure that the user reports.

trajectory_signals: unit-test pass before manual browser report
screenshot or DOM state after Add
decision_signals: supervisor attribution to missing browser validation
decision contract for adding e2e coverage

intervention_trace.summary: The user manually opened the app, clicked Add, and reported that the empty-state message still overlaps the first todo item.
evidence: manual browser note: empty state overlaps first item after Add
visible unit tests passed before the user intervention

decision_contract.hypothesis: The failure repeats because the harness exposes unit tests but not browser-observable UI behavior.
decision_contract.expected_effect: Adding browser validation to the harness should let a replaying worker catch and fix the overlap without user browser intervention.
decision_contract.falsification_check: Replay still requires a manual browser report or e2e validation remains absent.

permissions: supervisor may add browser harness and e2e tests
worker may edit application code during replay
hidden e2e assertions must not be shown to the worker
information_boundaries: manual intervention summary may be converted into public acceptance criteria
hidden check implementation must remain scorer-only

replay_protocol.replay: run worker from the original user_request with the repaired browser harness enabled
success_criteria: no fresh human browser intervention is required

notes: Seed card for benchmark design; executable fixture still needs to be built.
